seoTitle: How to Edit Audio in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to edit audio in Canva, including trimming, adjusting volume, and adding music or voiceovers to your video projects.
intro: Canva includes basic audio editing tools that let you shape sound directly within your video project, without needing separate audio software. This is useful for adding music, voiceovers, or sound effects to social videos, presentations, and promotional content. This guide covers the core editing steps.

steps.heading: Upload or select an audio track
steps.body: Add your own audio file or choose one from Canva's built-in music library, then drag it onto your project's timeline.

steps.heading: Trim the clip to length
steps.body: Drag the edges of the audio clip on the timeline to cut it down to the section you need.

steps.heading: Adjust the volume
steps.body: Use the volume control to raise or lower the audio level, especially if you're layering it under a voiceover.

steps.heading: Add effects or fades
steps.body: Apply a fade-in or fade-out so the audio starts and ends smoothly rather than cutting abruptly.

steps.heading: Preview and export your project
steps.body: Play back the full video to check the audio timing, then export your finished design.

faq.question: Can I add a voiceover directly in Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva has a built-in voiceover recording tool you can use alongside your uploaded audio.

faq.question: Can I layer multiple audio tracks together?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add several audio clips to different points on the timeline, including background music and voiceover at once.

faq.question: Is Canva's music library free to use?
faq.answer: Many tracks are free, while some premium music requires a Canva Pro subscription.

faq.question: Can I mute the original video sound and keep my new audio?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust or remove a video clip's original audio independently of any tracks you add.
